steps.description: Revise the backend file upload route contract to match the requested API precisely and the current frontend/tests: keep auth + UUID/Zod validation, dangerous extension blocking, 1GB max size, S3 presign flow with 15-minute expiry, and local dev multipart upload support; fix local upload parsing/response shape issues highlighted by prior feedback; ensure the route exports remain compatible because this file is not a hub.

Wire the files route into the Express app and ensure local uploads are actually served as static assets in dev/local mode using the configured uploads directory/base URL so GET metadata returns a usable download URL for embedding in the editor.

Revise the comments route permission model and response contract to eliminate the reported 403 on POST /api/documents/:id/comments, while preserving UUID validation, thread grouping by comment_id, reply handling via parent_id, update/resolve/delete semantics, and author/admin delete rules. This step should focus on document/workspace access checks and any contract mismatches with current consumers/tests.

Confirm and, if needed, align backlinks behavior with the requested contract for listing source documents and syncing document_links via upsert from extracted mentions/links, keeping UUID validation and workspace scoping consistent with the comments/files route adjustments.

Update OpenAPI schemas so the documented files/comments/backlinks contracts match the implemented API exactly, including upload-request response shape, local multipart upload response, file metadata response, comment thread list/create/update/resolve/delete payloads, and backlink/link sync payloads.

Bring the frontend upload service into contract alignment with the backend upload-request/upload/file metadata endpoints: support presigned S3 PUTs and local multipart POSTs, normalize the returned file metadata URL shape for editor embedding, and keep progress tracking integrated with the upload tracker utilities.

Ensure upload tracking and navigation warning behavior are coherent with the file upload flow, including active upload state updates, beforeunload warnings, and the warning component state/UI used by the app while uploads are in flight.

Update editor upload integrations so uploaded image/file URLs returned from the API are embedded/rendered correctly in TipTap for both image uploads and file attachments, using the revised upload service contract and keeping progress/navigation tracking intact.

Refactor the comments TanStack Query hook to use the actual document/comments API contract rather than stale post/replies endpoints, exposing fetch/create/reply/update/resolve/delete mutations that map to document threads and resolved_at correctly.

Align the inline comment TipTap integrations with the revised comments hook/types and backend contract so comment marks, inline thread display, replies, resolve actions, and resolved thread rendering all operate on document comment threads correctly.

Update backend route tests for files/comments/backlinks to match the intended contracts and the prior feedback: fix the comments POST permission/setup expectations, correct the local upload contract assertions, and remove stale assumptions such as file record deletion when the current behavior is metadata persistence.

Update end-to-end coverage for the requested verification flows so tests reflect the real API/UI contracts: upload an image through the files API, confirm the returned URL is valid and renders in TipTap, and verify inline comment thread creation, reply, resolve, and resolved_at persistence.

     3	test.describe('Inline Comments', () => {
     4	  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
     5	    await page.goto('/login')
     6	    await page.locator('#email').fill('dev@ship.local')
     7	    await page.locator('#password').fill('admin123')
     8	    await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Sign in', exact: true }).click()
     9	    await expect(page).not.toHaveURL('/login', { timeout: 5000 })
    10	  })
    11	
    12	  /**
    13	   * Helper: create a document, type text, and return the document page
    14	   */
    15	  async function createDocumentWithText(page: any, text: string) {
    16	    await page.goto('/docs')
    17	    const newButton = page.getByRole('button', { name: 'New Document', exact: true })
    18	    await expect(newButton).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
    19	    await newButton.click()
    20	    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/documents\/[a-f0-9-]+/, { timeout: 10000 })
    21	    await expect(page.locator('.ProseMirror')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
    22	
    23	    // Type text into editor
    24	    const editor = page.locator('.ProseMirror')
    25	    await editor.click()
    26	    await page.keyboard.type(text, { delay: 5 })
    27	    // Wait for content to sync
    28	    await page.waitForTimeout(500)
    29	  }
    30	
    31	  /**
    32	   * Helper: select a specific substring within the editor paragraph
    33	   */
    34	  async function selectText(page: any, target: string) {
    35	    await page.evaluate((t: string) => {
    36	      const p = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tiptap-editor"] .ProseMirror p')
    37	      if (!p) return
    38	      const text = p.textContent || ''
    39	      const idx = text.indexOf(t)
    40	      if (idx === -1) return
    41	      const walker = document.createTreeWalker(p, NodeFilter.SHOW_TEXT)
    42	      let node: Text | null
    43	      let offset = 0
    44	      while ((node = walker.nextNode() as Text | null)) {
    45	        const len = node.textContent?.length || 0
    46	        if (offset + len > idx) {
    47	          const range = document.createRange()
    48	          range.setStart(node, idx - offset)
    49	          range.setEnd(node, idx - offset + t.length)
    50	          window.getSelection()?.removeAllRanges()
    51	          window.getSelection()?.addRange(range)
    52	          break
    53	        }
    54	        offset += len
    55	      }
    56	    }, target)
    57	    await page.waitForTimeout(400)
    58	  }
    59	
    60	  test('bubble menu shows Comment button on text selection', async ({ page }) => {
    61	    await createDocumentWithText(page, 'Select this text to see the comment button appear.')
    62	
    63	    await selectText(page, 'this text')
    64	
    65	    // BubbleMenu should appear with Comment button
    66	    const commentBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Comment' })
    67	    await expect(commentBtn).toBeVisible({ timeout: 3000 })
    68	  })
    69	
    70	  test('can create an inline comment via bubble menu', async ({ page }) => {
    71	    await createDocumentWithText(page, 'This paragraph has text that will receive an inline comment.')
    72	
    73	    await selectText(page, 'inline comment')
    74	
    75	    // Click Comment in bubble menu
    76	    await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Comment' }).click()
    77	
    78	    // Comment input should appear
    79	    const commentInput = page.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Write a comment...' })
    80	    await expect(commentInput).toBeVisible({ timeout: 3000 })
    81	
    82	    // Type and submit
    83	    await commentInput.fill('This is a test comment')
    84	    await commentInput.press('Enter')
    85	
    86	    // Wait for API response and decoration re-render
    87	    await page.waitForTimeout(1500)
    88	
    89	    // The inline comment card should appear with the comment text
    90	    await expect(page.getByText('This is a test comment')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
    91	
    92	    // The highlighted text should have the comment-highlight class
    93	    const highlight = page.locator('.comment-highlight')
    94	    await expect(highlight).toBeVisible()
    95	  })
    96	
    97	  test('can create a comment via Cmd+Shift+M keyboard shortcut', async ({ page }) => {
    98	    await createDocumentWithText(page, 'Testing keyboard shortcut for adding comments quickly.')
    99	
   100	    await selectText(page, 'keyboard shortcut')
   101	
   102	    // Press Cmd+Shift+M
   103	    await page.keyboard.press('Meta+Shift+m')
   104	
   105	    // Comment input should appear

   118	  test('canceling a comment removes the highlight', async ({ page }) => {
   119	    await createDocumentWithText(page, 'This text will have a comment that gets canceled.')
   120

   122

    10	
    11	export const UploadRequestSchema = z.object({

    13	  mimeType: z.string().min(1).max(100),
    14	  sizeBytes: z.number().int().positive().max(1024 * 1024 * 1024),
    15	}).openapi('UploadRequest');
    16	registry.register('UploadRequest', UploadRequestSchema);
    17	
    18	export const UploadResponseSchema = z.object({

    20	  filename: z.string(),

    23	  status: FileStatusSchema,

    26	  method: z.enum(['PUT', 'POST']),
    27	  expiresInSeconds: z.number().int().nullable(),
    28	  storageKey: z.string(),
    29	  uploadEndpoint: z.string().nullable().optional(),
    30	}).openapi('UploadResponse');
    31	registry.register('UploadResponse', UploadResponseSchema);
    32

    34	  id: UuidSchema,
    35	  filename: z.string(),
    36	  status: FileStatusSchema,
    37	  downloadUrl: z.string().url(),
    38	}).openapi('LocalUploadResponse');
    39	registry.register('LocalUploadResponse', LocalUploadResponseSchema);
    40	
    41	export const FileResponseSchema = z.object({

    50	  errorMessage: z.string().nullable(),

    53	registry.register('FileResponse', FileResponseSchema);
    54

    "docker:clean": "docker compose -f docker-compose.local.yml down -v",
    "dev:raw": "pnpm --parallel --recursive run dev",
    "dev:api": "pnpm --filter @ship/api dev",
    "dev:web": "pnpm --filter @ship/web dev",
    "dev:shared": "pnpm --filter @ship/shared dev",

    "test:e2e": "playwright test",

    "test:e2e:preflight": "node scripts/playwright-preflight.mjs",

{"success":true,"exit_code":0,"stdout":"# Manual Verification Notes

Use this checklist when validating the new upload, image embedding, and comment lifecycle flows in a local dev or preview environment.

## File upload API
- [ ] Sign in as a normal user.
- [ ] Request `/api/files/upload` from the browser or API client.
- [ ] Confirm the response returns a usable `uploadUrl` plus `fileId` and `s3Key`.
- [ ] If the environment uses local file storage, upload the file to the returned local endpoint.
- [ ] Confirm `GET /api/files/:id` returns a persisted `cdn_url` that is not empty and is usable by the editor.

## TipTap image embedding
- [ ] Open a new document.
- [ ] Insert an image through the editor flow.
- [ ] Confirm the image renders immediately in TipTap.
- [ ] Confirm the temporary preview is replaced by a persisted URL (`http(s)://...` or `/api/files/...`).
- [ ] Reload the page and confirm the image still renders after refresh.

## Comment thread lifecycle
- [ ] Create a root comment on a document.
- [ ] Add a reply using `parent_id`.
- [ ] Verify the thread groups under a single `comment_id` and includes both comments.
- [ ] Resolve the thread.
- [ ] Confirm the resolved comment response includes `resolved_at`.
- [ ] Reload the thread list and confirm `resolved_at` is still present.

## Notes
- The automated coverage for these checks lives in:
  - `e2e/file-upload-api.spec.ts`
  - `e2e/images.spec.ts`
  - `api/src/routes/comments.test.ts`

    15	  async function createDocumentWithText(page: any, text: string) {
    16	    await page.goto('/docs')
    17	    const newButton = page.getByRole('button', { name: 'New Document', exact: true })
    18	    await expect(newButton).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
    19	    await newButton.click()
    20	    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/documents\/[a-f0-9-]+/, { timeout: 10000 })
    21	    await expect(page.locator('.ProseMirror')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })

    23	    // Type text into editor
    24	    const editor = page.locator('.ProseMirror')
    25	    await editor.click()
    26	    await page.keyboard.type(text, { delay: 5 })
    27	    // Wait for content to sync
    28	    await page.waitForTimeout(500)
    29	  }
    30	
    31	  /**
    32	   * Helper: select a specific substring within the editor paragraph
    33	   */
    34	  async function selectText(page: any, target: string) {
    35	    await page.evaluate((t: string) => {
    36	      const p = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tiptap-editor"] .ProseMirror p')
    37	      if (!p) return
    38	      const text = p.textContent || ''
    39	      const idx = text.indexOf(t)
    40	      if (idx === -1) return
    41	      const walker = document.createTreeWalker(p, NodeFilter.SHOW_TEXT)
    42	      let node: Text | null
    43	      let offset = 0
    44	      while ((node = walker.nextNode() as Text | null)) {
    45	        const len = node.textContent?.length || 0
    46	        if (offset + len > idx) {
    47	          const range = document.createRange()
    48	          range.setStart(node, idx - offset)
    49	          range.setEnd(node, idx - offset + t.length)
    50	          window.getSelection()?.removeAllRanges()
    51	          window.getSelection()?.addRange(range)
    52	          break
    53	        }
    54	        offset += len
    55	      }
    56	    }, target)
    57	    await page.waitForTimeout(400)
    58	  }

tool_input.label: comments-api-blocker
tool_input.content: The comments route implementation appears to require workspaceId on auth context for session-authenticated requests. In the current test, POST /api/documents/:id/comments is still returning 403 even after fixing document creation and removing an invalid comment_id payload for root threads. The failure occurs before assertions on returned body, so the likely issue is auth/ownership gating in the route or request context rather than reply/thread shape.

{"success":true,"content":"     1	/**
     2	 * File upload schemas - Request upload credentials, local upload, and file metadata
     3	 */
     4	
     5	import { z, registry } from '../registry.js';
     6	import { UuidSchema, DateTimeSchema } from './common.js';
     7	
     8	export const FileStatusSchema = z.enum(['pending', 'processing', 'uploaded', 'failed']).openapi('FileStatus');
     9	registry.register('FileStatus', FileStatusSchema);
    10	
    11	export const UploadRequestSchema = z.object({
    12	  filename: z.string().min(1).max(255),
    13	  mimeType: z.string().min(1).max(100),
    14	  sizeBytes: z.number().int().positive().max(1024 * 1024 * 1024),
    15	}).openapi('UploadRequest');
    16	registry.register('UploadRequest', UploadRequestSchema);
    17	
    18	export const UploadResponseSchema = z.object({
    19	  id: UuidSchema,
    20	  filename: z.string(),
    21	  mimeType: z.string(),
    22	  sizeBytes: z.number().int(),
    23	  status: FileStatusSchema,
    24	  downloadUrl: z.string().nullable(),
    25	  uploadUrl: z.string().nullable(),
    26	  method: z.enum(['PUT', 'POST']),
    27	  expiresInSeconds: z.number().int().nullable(),
    28	  storageKey: z.string(),
    29	  uploadEndpoint: z.string().nullable().optional(),
    30	}).openapi('UploadResponse');
    31	registry.register('UploadResponse', UploadResponseSchema);
    32	
    33	export const LocalUploadResponseSchema = z.object({
    34	  id: UuidSchema,
    35	  filename: z.string(),
    36	  status: FileStatusSchema,
    37	  downloadUrl: z.string().url(),
    38	}).openapi('LocalUploadResponse');
    39	registry.register('LocalUploadResponse', LocalUploadResponseSchema);
    40	
    41	export const FileResponseSchema = z.object({
    42	  id: UuidSchema,
    43	  filename: z.string(),
    44	  mimeType: z.string(),
    45	  sizeBytes: z.number(),
    46	  status: FileStatusSchema,
    47	  createdAt: DateTimeSchema,
    48	  uploadedAt: DateTimeSchema.nullable(),
    49	  failedAt: DateTimeSchema.nullable(),
    50	  errorMessage: z.string().nullable(),
    51	  downloadUrl: z.string(),
    52	}).openapi('FileResponse');
    53	registry.register('FileResponse', FileResponseSchema);
    54	
    55	registry.registerPath({
    56	  method: 'post',
    57	  path: '/api/files/upload-request',
    58	  operationId: 'create_file_upload_request',
    59	  summary: 'Create a file upload request',
    60	  tags: ['Files'],
    61	  request: { body: { content: { 'application/json': { schema: UploadRequestSchema } } } },
    62	  responses: {
    63	    201: { description: 'Upload request created', content: { 'application/json': { schema: UploadResponseSchema } } },
    64	    400: { description: 'Invalid upload request' },
    65	  },
    66	});
    67	
    68	registry.registerPath({
    69	  method: 'post',
    70	  path: '/api/files/upload/{id}',
    71	  operationId: 'upload_file_local',
    72	  summary: 'Upload a file locally',
    73	  tags: ['Files'],
    74	  request: {
    75	    params: z.object({ id: UuidSchema }),
    76	    body: {
    77	      content: {
    78	        'multipart/form-data': {
    79	          schema: z.object({ file: z.any() }).openapi('LocalFileUploadRequest'),
    80	        },
    81	      },
    82	    },
    83	  },
    84	  responses: {
    85	    200: { description: 'File uploaded', content: { 'application/json': { schema: LocalUploadResponseSchema } } },
    86	    400: { description: 'Invalid upload request' },
    87	    404: { description: 'File not found' },
    88	  },
    89	});
    90	
    91	registry.registerPath({
    92	  method: 'get',
    93	  path: '/api/files/{id}',
    94	  operationId: 'get_file',
    95	  summary: 'Get file metadata',
    96	  tags: ['Files'],
    97	  request: { params: z.object({ id: UuidSchema }) },
    98	  responses: {
    99	    200: { description: 'File metadata', content: { 'application/json': { schema: FileResponseSchema } } },
   100	    404: { description: 'File not found' },
   101	  },
   102	});

     2	import { triggerMentionPopup } from './fixtures/test-helpers'
     3	
     4	/**
     5	 * Backlinks E2E Tests
     6	 *
     7	 * Tests backlink panel display, creation, removal, and navigation.
     8	 */
     9	
    10	// Helper to login before each test
    11	async function login(page: Page) {
    12	  await page.goto('/login')
    13	  await page.locator('#email').fill('dev@ship.local')
    14	  await page.locator('#password').fill('admin123')
    15	  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Sign in', exact: true }).click()
    16	  await expect(page).not.toHaveURL('/login', { timeout: 5000 })
    17	}
    18	
    19	// Helper to create a new document and get to the editor
    20	async function createNewDocument(page: Page) {
    21	  await page.goto('/docs')
    22	  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'New Document', exact: true }).click()
    23	  await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/documents\/[a-f0-9-]+/, { timeout: 10000 })
    24	  await expect(page.locator('.ProseMirror')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
    25	  return page.url()
    26	}
    27	
    28	// Helper to set document title
    29	async function setDocumentTitle(page: Page, title: string) {
    30	  const titleInput = page.getByPlaceholder('Untitled')
    31	  await expect(titleInput).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
    32	  await titleInput.fill(title)
    33	  await page.waitForResponse(
    34	    resp => resp.url().includes('/api/documents/') && resp.request().method() === 'PATCH',
    35	    { timeout: 5000 }
    36	  )
    37	  await page.waitForTimeout(500)
    38	}
    39	
    40	test.describe('Backlinks', () => {
    41	  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
    42	    await login(page)
    43	  })
    44	
    45	  test('backlinks panel shows in sidebar', async ({ page }) => {
    46	    await createNewDocument(page)
    47	
    48	    // Look for backlinks panel in properties sidebar (right side)
    49	    // Common selectors: "Backlinks", "Referenced by", or a data attribute
    50	    const backlinksPanel = page.locator('text="Backlinks"').or(
    51	      page.locator('text="Referenced by"')
    52	    ).or(
    53	      page.locator('[data-backlinks-panel]')
    54	    )
    55	
    56	    // Backlinks panel should be visible in sidebar
    57	    await expect(backlinksPanel.first()).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
    58	  })
    59	
    60	  test('creating mention adds backlink', async ({ page }) => {
    61	    // Create Document A (will be mentioned)
    62	    const docAUrl = await createNewDocument(page)
    63	    await setDocumentTitle(page, 'Document A')
    64	
    65	    // Create Document B (will mention Document A)
    66	    const docBUrl = await createNewDocument(page)
    67	    await setDocumentTitle(page, 'Document B')
    68	
    69	    const editor = page.locator('.ProseMirror')
    70	    await triggerMentionPopup(page, editor)
    71	
    72	    // Type search term to filter
    73	    await page.keyboard.type('Document A')
    74	    await page.waitForTimeout(500)
    75	
    76	    // Select Document A from list
    77	    const docAOption = page.locator('[role="option"]').filter({ hasText: 'Document A' })
    78	    await expect(docAOption).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
    79	    await docAOption.click()
    80	    await page.waitForTimeout(1000)
    81	
    82	    // Navigate to Document A
    83	    await page.goto(docAUrl)
    84	    await expect(page.locator('.ProseMirror')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
    85	    await page.waitForTimeout(1000)
    86	
    87	    // Reload to ensure backlinks are fetched fresh
    88	    await page.reload()
    89	    await expect(page.locator('.ProseMirror')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
    90	    await page.waitForTimeout(1000)
    91	
    92	    // Document A should now show Document B in backlinks
    93	    // Scope to properties sidebar to avoid matching sidebar doc list
    94	    const propertiesSidebar = page.locator('aside[aria-label="Document properties"]')
    95	    await expect(propertiesSidebar).toBeVisible({ timeout: 3000 })
    96	
    97	    const backlinksPanel = propertiesSidebar.locator('text="Backlinks"').or(
    98	      propertiesSidebar.locator('text="Referenced by"')
    99	    ).or(
   100	      propertiesSidebar.locator('[data-backlinks-panel]')
   101	    ).first()
   102	
   103	    await expect(backlinksPanel).toBeVisible({ timeout: 3000 })
   104	
   105	    // Look for Document B in backlinks (within properties sidebar)
   106	    const hasDocB = await propertiesSidebar.locator('text="Document B"').isVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
   107	    expect(hasDocB).toBeTruthy()
   108	  })
   109	
   110	  test('removing mention removes backlink', async ({ page }) => {
   111	    // Create Document A (will be mentioned)
   112	    const docAUrl = await createNewDocument(page)
   113	    await setDocumentTitle(page, 'Doc to Mention')
   114	
   115	    // Create Document B (will mention Document A, then remove it)
   116	    const docBUrl = await createNewDocument(page)
   117	    await setDocumentTitle(page, 'Doc with Mention')
   118	
   119	    const editor = page.locator('.ProseMirror')
   120	    await triggerMentionPopup(page, editor)
   121	
   122	    await page.keyboard.type('Doc to Mention')
   123	    await page.waitForTimeout(500)
   124	
   125	    const docOption = page.locator('[role="option"]').filter({ hasText: 'Doc to Mention' })
   126	    await expect(docOption).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
   127	    await docOption.click()
   128	    await page.waitForTimeout(1000)
   129	
   130	    // Delete the mention by selecting all content and deleting it
   131	    // NOTE: Can't click on .mention directly because MentionExtension's click handler
   132	    // calls onNavigate() which navigates away from the page.
   133	    // Instead, use keyboard shortcuts to select all and delete.
   134	    const mention = editor.locator('.mention')
   135	    await expect(mention).toBeVisible({ timeout: 3000 })
   136	
   137	    // Focus the editor and select all content
   138	    await editor.click()
   139	    await page.keyboard.press('Meta+a') // Select all (Cmd+A on Mac)
   140	    await page.keyboard.press('Backspace') // Delete selected content
   141	
   142	    // Wait for editor update to propagate (debounce is 500ms)
   143	    await page.waitForTimeout(1000)
   144	
   145	    // Verify mention is deleted before waiting for POST
   146	    await expect(editor.locator('.mention')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 3000 })
   147	
   148	    // Wait for the link sync POST request (debounced 500ms)
   149	    await page.waitForResponse(
   150	      resp => resp.url().includes('/links') && resp.request().method() === 'POST',
   151	      { timeout: 5000 }
   152	    ).catch((err) => {
   153	      console.log('No /links POST detected after mention removal:', err.message)
   154	    })
   155	
   156	    // Extra wait for sync to propagate
   157	    await page.waitForTimeout(1500)
   158	
   159	    // Navigate to Document A and reload to ensure fresh backlinks data
   160	    await page.goto(docAUrl)
   161	    await expect(page.locator('.ProseMirror')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
   162	    await page.waitForTimeout(500)
   163	
   164	    // Reload to ensure backlinks are fetched fresh from server
   165	    await page.reload()
   166	    await expect(page.locator('.ProseMirror')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
   167	    await page.waitForTimeout(1000)
   168	
   169	    // Document A should NOT show Document B in backlinks (or show empty state)
   170	    // Look within the properties sidebar for backlinks
   171	    const propertiesSidebar = page.locator('aside[aria-label="Document properties"]')
   172	    await expect(propertiesSidebar).toBeVisible({ timeout: 3000 })
   173	
   174	    // Should either show "No backlinks" or not have "Doc with Mention" in the backlinks section
   175	    const hasNoBacklinks = await propertiesSidebar.locator('text="No backlinks"').isVisible({ timeout: 2000 })
   176	    const hasDocWithMention = await propertiesSidebar.locator('text="Doc with Mention"').isVisible({ timeout: 2000 })
   177	
   178	    // Either "No backlinks" is shown, OR the doc is not in the backlinks
   179	    expect(hasNoBacklinks || !hasDocWithMention).toBeTruthy()
   180	  })
   181	
   182	  test('backlinks show correct document info', async ({ page }) => {
   183	    // Create Document X
   184	    const docXUrl = await createNewDocument(page)
   185	    await setDocumentTitle(page, 'Target Document')
   186	
   187	    // Create Document Y that mentions X
   188	    const docYUrl = await createNewDocument(page)

   190	
   191	    const editor = page.locator('.ProseMirror')
   192	    await triggerMentionPopup(page, editor)
   193	
   194	    await page.keyboard.type('Target Document')
   195	    await page.waitForTimeout(500)
   196	
   197	    const docOption = page.locator('[role="option"]').filter({ hasText: 'Target Document' })
   198	    await expect(docOption).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })
   199	    await docOption.click()
   200	    await page.waitForTimeout(1000)
   201	
   202	    // Navigate to Target Document
   203	    await page.goto(docXUrl)
   204	    await expect(page.locator('.ProseMirror')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 })

   206

messages.content:
Implement file upload and inline comments systems for Ship.

For file uploads, create `routes/files.ts` with auth middleware: POST /api/files/upload-request (validates filename/mimeType/sizeBytes with Zod, blocks dangerous extensions like .exe/.bat/.sh/.dll/.jar/.ps1, creates a files row with status=pending, returns a presigned S3 upload URL if S3_UPLOADS_BUCKET is configured or a local upload endpoint for dev), POST /api/files/upload/:id (for local dev: accepts multipart form data, writes to uploads/ directory, updates files row to status=uploaded), GET /api/files/:id (returns file metadata + download URL). For S3: use @aws-sdk/client-s3 and @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner with 15-minute URL expiration. For local dev: serve uploads/ as static files. Set max file size to 1GB. Validate UUID format on all ID params to prevent path traversal.

For comments, create `routes/comments.ts` with: GET /api/documents/:id/comments (list all comment threads for a document, grouped by comment_id), POST /api/documents/:id/comments (create a new comment thread with comment_id matching the TipTap comment mark, or reply to existing via parent_id), PATCH /api/comments/:id (update comment content), POST /api/comments/:id/resolve (set resolved_at timestamp), DELETE /api/comments/:id (only author or admin can delete). Create `routes/backlinks.ts` with: GET /api/documents/:id/backlinks (query document_links where target_id matches, return source documents with title and type), POST /api/documents/:id/links (upsert document_links from extracted mentions/links in content).

On the frontend, create `src/services/upload.ts` with: requestUpload (calls upload-request endpoint), uploadFile (either uploads directly to presigned S3 URL or to the local endpoint), trackUploadProgress. Create `src/services/uploadTracker.ts` to track active uploads and warn on navigation (UploadNavigationWarning component). Create `src/hooks/useCommentsQuery.ts` for fetching and mutating comments via TanStack Query.

Verify: upload an image via the API, confirm it returns a valid URL, embed it in a TipTap document via the editor, confirm it renders. Create a comment thread on a document, reply to it, resolve it, confirm the resolved_at timestamp is set.
